_publications._publicationTitle: Combined Inhibition of SHP2 and MEK Is Effective in Models of NF1-Deficient Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumors

details: JH-2-002 PDX used in in vivo subcutaneous xenograft study; combined trametinib (MEKi) + SHP099/RMC-4550 (SHP2i) produced sustained tumor growth inhibition (>28 days) with enhanced Ki-67 reduction and suppression of ERK/DUSP6 rebound, outperforming either monotherapy; demonstrates that RAS-GTP reactivation through RTK-mediated feedback limits single-agent MEKi efficacy in NF1-MPNST
